instructions: 1) Update the `plan` and `run` commands in `src/jitsu/cli/main.py` to accept a `verbose: Annotated[bool, typer.Option('--verbose', '-v', help='Enable verbose debug output.')] = False` parameter. 2) Update `_run_planner` signature to accept `verbose: bool = False` and pass the flag into it from the commands. 3) In `_run_planner`, update the `InstructorRetryException` block to print `e` and `e.__cause__` to stderr. 4) CRITICAL: To avoid Ruff C901 (complexity > 10), extract the exception handling block inside `_run_planner` into a separate helper function (e.g., `_handle_planner_error(e: Exception, verbose: bool)`). 5) Update `tests/cli/test_main.py` to cover the new flag.
anti_patterns: Do not exceed McCabe complexity 10 in any function.
Do not break existing CLI command signatures; ensure `verbose` defaults to False.
completion_criteria: The `--verbose` flag is added to `plan` and `run`.
Error handling is extracted to keep `_run_planner` complexity low.
CLI tests pass with 100% coverage and zero Ruff errors.

instructions: 1) Update `JitsuPlanner.generate_plan` in `src/jitsu/core/planner.py` to accept a `verbose: bool = False` argument. Make sure `_run_planner` passes this in. 2) Inside `generate_plan`, if `verbose` is True, use `typer.secho(..., err=True)` to print the JSON string of the `EpicBlueprint` immediately after Pass 1 completes. 3) Similarly, if `verbose` is True, print the JSON string of each `AgentDirective` immediately after it is generated in Pass 2. 4) Update `tests/core/test_planner.py` to pass the `verbose` argument in tests and maintain 100% test coverage.
anti_patterns: Do not use standard `print()`; always use `typer.secho(..., err=True)` to respect the stdio boundary.
completion_criteria: `generate_plan` accepts `verbose=True` and outputs JSON dumps of the models to stderr.
